props.title: Spencer On Private Relationship Memory
props.subtitle: Personal CRM, agentic coding loops, UX QA, and the atomized developer problem

cards.title: Private relationship memory
cards.body: Spencer is building a self-hosted personal CRM to keep relationship context private while remembering birthdays, life events, follow-ups, and relationship history.

cards.title: Doer reviewer loop
cards.body: Claude Code coordinates the work, Codex-style reviewers check plans and implementations, and deterministic checks backstop the process.

cards.title: Context and verification
cards.body: The hard questions were context and trust: how do agents know enough, and how do humans know the agent did good work?

cards.title: UX QA bottleneck
cards.body: Spencer is exploring agents that inspect human-facing interfaces, open issues, and eventually help fix what they find.

cards.title: The atomized developer
cards.body: Agents may make individuals more capable, but weaken the shared context, relationships, and collaboration that make project work meaningful.
